originalTitle: Social Security cost-of-living adjustment estimate for 2027 falls as inflation cools
summary: New estimates show Social Security's annual cost-of-living adjustment for 2027 may be between 3.7% and 3.8%. Here's what to know.
content:
Cooling inflation has also chilled some experts' expectations for the 2027 Social Security cost-of-living adjustment, which may be between 3.7% and 3.8%, according to new estimates.

The Social Security COLA may be 3.7% in 2027, estimates Mary Johnson, an independent Social Security and Medicare analyst. That figure is 1 full percentage point below the 4.7% COLA for 2027 Johnson estimated last month.

"This is a significant drop in inflation, and one that we've rarely seen in the June CPI data over the past five years," Johnson said in a statement.

New government data released Tuesday shows the consumer price index increased 3.5% over the past 12 months as of June. That was lower than expectations due to a decline in energy prices.

Meanwhile, the Senior Citizens League, a nonpartisan senior group, estimates the Social Security COLA for 2027 may be 3.8% , unchanged from its projection from last month.

To be sure, those estimates are subject to change. The official COLA for the following year is typically announced by the Social Security Administration in October.

Social Security benefits are adjusted annually through cost-of-living adjustments to help those monthly checks keep pace with inflation.

The annual COLA has averaged 3.1% over the past 10 years, according to the Social Security Administration. In 2026, more than 75 million Social Security and Supplemental Security Income beneficiaries saw a 2.8% increase to their benefits.

How Medicare premiums may change in 2027

Retirement confidence among retirees has declined, according to a January survey fielded by the Employee Benefit Research Institute, a nonprofit, nonpartisan research firm; and Greenwald Research, a research consulting firm.

Retirees' confidence fell 5 percentage points to 73%, the survey, which included 1,045 individuals in retirement, found. Top worries included inflation, debt, healthcare costs, housing expenses and potential changes to the retirement system, according to the results. Two in 5 retirees said their healthcare costs have been higher than expected, the survey found.

Read more CNBC personal finance coverage

One healthcare expense — standard premiums for Medicare Part B , which covers medically necessary and preventive services — may be $209.50 per month in 2027, according to estimates in the annual Medicare trustees report released in June. That is up from $202.90 per month in 2026 — an increase of $6.60 or 3.3%. Higher-income beneficiaries may pay additional surcharges.

"That's relatively low," Johnson said. "The average increase in Part B is usually about 5.4% per year, and that's what it's been over the past 10 years."

The initial deductible for Medicare Part D, which covers prescription drugs, will be $700 in 2027, up from $615 in 2026, according to the trustees report . Meanwhile, the catastrophic threshold, or out-of-pocket spending limit, will be set at $2,400 next year, up from $2,100 in 2026. Those new thresholds have been finalized, according to the report.

Correction: Standard premiums for Medicare Part B in 2027 would be up from $202.90 per month in 2026 — an increase of $6.60 or 3.3%. An earlier version mischaracterized the figures.
chineseTitle: 随着通胀降温，2027 年社会保障生活成本调整预估下降
chineseSummary: 新的估计显示，2027 年社会保障年度生活费用调整可能在 3.7% 至 3.8% 之间。以下是需要了解的内容。
chineseContent:
通胀降温也降低了一些专家对2027年社会保障生活成本调整的预期，根据新的估计，这一调整可能在3.7%至3.8%之间。

独立社会保障和医疗保险分析师玛丽·约翰逊 (Mary Johnson) 估计，到 2027 年，社会保障 COLA 可能会达到 3.7%。这个数字比约翰逊上个月估计的 2027 年可乐 4.7% 低了整整 1 个百分点。

约翰逊在一份声明中表示：“通胀大幅下降，这是我们过去五年来在 6 月 CPI 数据中很少见到的情况。”

周二公布的新政府数据显示，截至 6 月，过去 12 个月消费者价格指数上涨 3.5%。由于能源价格下跌，这一数字低于预期。

与此同时，无党派老年人团体老年人联盟估计 2027 年社会保障 COLA 可能为 3.8%，与上个月的预测持平。

可以肯定的是，这些估计可能会发生变化。下一年的官方 COLA 通常由社会保障管理局在 10 月份公布。

社会保障福利每年通过生活成本调整进行调整，以帮助每月支票跟上通货膨胀的步伐。

根据社会保障管理局的数据，过去 10 年，每年的 COLA 平均为 3.1%。 2026 年，超过 7500 万社会保障和补充保障收入受益人的福利增加了 2.8%。

2027 年 Medicare 保费可能会发生怎样的变化

非营利、无党派研究公司员工福利研究所一月份进行的一项调查显示，退休人员的退休信心有所下降；以及格林沃尔德研究公司（Greenwald Research），一家研究咨询公司。

这项针对 1,045 名退休人员的调查发现，退休人员的信心下降了 5 个百分点，至 73%。结果显示，最令人担忧的问题包括通货膨胀、债务、医疗费用、住房费用以及退休制度的潜在变化。调查发现，五分之二的退休人员表示，他们的医疗费用高于预期。

阅读更多 CNBC 个人理财报道

根据 6 月份发布的年度 Medicare 受托人报告中的估计，一项医疗保健费用 - Medicare B 部分的标准保费（涵盖医疗必需和预防性服务）到 2027 年可能为每月 209.50 美元。这比 2026 年的每月 202.90 美元有所增加，增加了 6.60 美元，即 3.3%。高收入受益人可能需要支付额外的附加费。

“这个比例相对较低，”约翰逊说。 “B部分的平均增幅通常为每年5.4%左右，过去10年就是这样。”

根据受托人报告，医疗保险 D 部分（涵盖处方药）的初始免赔额将从 2026 年的 615 美元增加到 2027 年的 700 美元。与此同时，灾难性门槛或自付费用限额将于明年定为 2,400 美元，高于 2026 年的 2,100 美元。报告称，这些新门槛已经最终确定。

更正：2027 年 Medicare B 部分的标准保费将从 2026 年的每月 202.90 美元上涨，增加 6.60 美元或 3.3%。早期版本错误地描述了这些数字。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
